body_txt: You want to symbolize a character as The Ingenue , Nice Girl , Cheerful Child , Girl Next Door , Kindhearted Simpleton , or show that she is Incorruptible Pure Pureness , and you're looking for a quick visual shorthand? Put her in a sundress. It's frequently topped with a Stylish Sunhat to add a classy feel to her. The sundress's reputation for conveying innocence is likely found in the fact that the garment is light, simple, and airy, with a flowing feel, allowing a female character to show off a feminine look without being Stripperific or going overboard with a Pimped-Out Dress in a place where it would seem too fancy. Areas of bare skin, like shoulders, back, or legs are possible, but generally nothing that would be considered immodest or showy. It also tends to be used in summer or spring settings, which tend to be ideal for Coming of Age love stories. What's more, while sundresses can come in a variety of prints and colors, white tends to be popular, often overlapping with Virgin in a White Dress . Sundresses can be simple or ornate, plain or with a print of some sort, but they are typically used to symbolize a character who is kind, sweet, innocent, and perhaps a trifle naive. The sundress may only show up in a Beach Episode , but it will signify a character's innocent nature. They're likely to be worn by the Girly Girl in a Tomboy and Girly Girl duo (unless the Tomboy lost a bet or the writers want to show that She Cleans Up Nicely ). Of course, some girls may choose a sundress precisely because of its association with innocence, though there are likely some who will avoid it for precisely that same reason, in some cases because they want a bad girl image, or in others because of a perception that they themselves are not innocent. It's also not unheard of to find this paired with someone who Prefers Going Barefoot , though a beach setting may also invite walking around barefoot, or at least in sandals.  And if you're going to be out in the sun, don't forget your Parasol of Prettiness ! If a sundress is backlit by the sun it may overlap with Sexy Silhouette , which may in turn cause it to overlap with Innocent Fanservice Girl or Reluctant Fanservice Girl . Can lead to an Accidental Pervert moment for anyone caught noticing, as well as a Luminescent Blush and/or Pervert Revenge Mode . Its normally innocent image has also been subverted with recent memes such as the "getting railed in a sundress" meme (i.e., the fantasy of rough sex while wearing one). And if she's crass or vulgar in her speech, it's Graceful Until She Speaks . If someone assumes a girl has an innocent personality because they find her in a sundress, they could discover that they are Loving a Shadow . This is an Always Female trope. Compare and contrast Pimped-Out Dress , which are favored by royalty and nobility. Also compare and contrast Sexy Slit Dress for another form of women's fashion that highlights femininity in a different way. Note: A female character in a sundress is not enough to qualify for this trope. There must be some connection to innocence, either real, perceived by other characters, or deliberately feigned.
